title: Good Evangelion games
wikitext:
[]: Good Evangelion games?             Quote Post by cojax8 » Mon Dec 07, 2009 10:59 pm I got GFOS a while back and played it all the way through and I enjoyed it, I got GFOS2 recently and played that all the way through and enjoyed it too, so, my question is. What are some good Eva games in your opinions? As I said before I’ve played GFOS1 and two and enjoyed them, I’m going through SIRP right now and, for the most part, seem to be enjoying it, so games along that line would be nice. The recommended games would have to be able to play on my comp, as the translation program I’m using doesn’t run on the psp, I’ve heard that psp games can be played on the comp, I would just need to figure out how too, and before I do, I would like some recommendations as to what games I should get.

[]:             Quote Post by Rock-Slash » Tue Dec 08, 2009 2:59 am Evangelion 64, is the only Eva game that I actually liked, you'll need a N64 emulator.

[]:             Quote Post by GVman » Thu Dec 24, 2009 10:26 pm Does anyone know anything about the ones on the Saturn?

[]:             Quote Post by scarmullet » Thu Dec 24, 2009 11:45 pm Ok, lets do some classifications, just because it is on a game console, does not make it a game The only Eva GAMES I have played were Eva 64 and Battle Orchestra. The rest seem to be simulators ie dating sims and such. Why cant EVA games come out in the US or atleast Europe.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Fri Dec 25, 2009 3:05 am Eva2 for PS2 and PSP (you know, the game with the CI? Anyway, it mixes battles/real world simulator/RPG/dating & raising sim) is the best, hands-down. PSP port especially.

[]:             Quote Post by AVman9 » Fri Dec 25, 2009 3:07 am Eva64 is excellent because it actually follows the story of the TV series. I do enjoy a good rumble with my friends on Battle Orchestra now and then, although the EVAs are a tad small when compared to their surroundings in that game.

[]:             Quote Post by Holy Diver » Sat Dec 26, 2009 10:10 pm I also want to get an Eva game or two, but I'm slighly put off by the thought of having to read moonrunes and the whole region lockout issue. I have a friend that ordered the Gundam game Operation Troy off the internet. The fact that he spent $50+shipping on it and that it was not compatable with his X-box 360, was probably the biggest (gaming related) dissapointment in his life.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sat Dec 26, 2009 11:07 pm There's not region lock-out with PSPs to play any of the games, hint hint. >_> Also, there is such a thing as walkthroughs for games, though depending on the game it can only help so much, yeah.

[]:             Quote Post by LordCaldwell » Tue Dec 29, 2009 6:51 pm Will any games ever be released in english?

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Tue Dec 29, 2009 6:57 pm So far they haven't been, so personally I doubt it although I guess time will tell.

[]:             Quote Post by GVman » Wed Dec 30, 2009 5:58 pm If Sakura Wars V is being given a release, anything is possible.

[]:             Quote Post by MisatoCrush » Mon Jan 27, 2014 7:32 am Sorry to dig up this old thread but I'd like some input from the experts...you! ^^ I'm really pining/yearning to see some shipping scenarios happen in a video game. Are there any role playing games or similar things where Shinji Asuka Misato and Rei interact and possibly go on dates and stuff? Like, basically, them involved in happy non-fighting scenarios? Or at least not focused on fighting the whole time?

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Mon Jan 27, 2014 12:02 pm If you want a game where the characters can go on dates/interact normally and includes Angel-Eva battles too, I recommend this: Sailor Star Dust wrote: Eva2 for PS2 and PSP (you know, the game with the CI? Anyway, it mixes battles/real world simulator/RPG/dating & raising sim) is the best, hands-down. PSP port especially.

What are some good Eva games in your opinions? As I said before I’ve played GFOS1 and two and enjoyed them, I’m going through SIRP right now and, for the most part, seem to be enjoyin
